**Student Profile for Incoming Teacher**

This student has a clear and confident grasp of grammatical logic — they articulated the full この・その・あの proximity system accurately and unprompted once they had a moment to reflect, suggesting strong conceptual retention when rules are explained spatially and explicitly. However, productive recall of irregular number forms remains their primary weak point: they dropped the geminate consonant in ろっぴゃく (producing ろぴゃく) and needed a correction cycle, consistent with the pattern flagged previously. Kana output has improved and is now largely accurate, but the teacher should still prompt for exact forms rather than accepting near-misses, as approximations (e.g. dropping small っ) are still likely under pressure. The student responds well to immediate, specific corrective feedback followed by a repeat-back prompt — this combination consistently produces the correct form on the second attempt, so short correction loops are effective and efficient. Continued drilling on the irregular hundreds and thousands (ろっぴゃく, はっぴゃく, さんびゃく, さんぜん, はっせん) is still recommended at the start of the next session, and a lightly playful or role-play framing continues to keep this student engaged and willing to attempt answers.
